title: Applied Mathematics Dissertation
outline: The course consists of the investigation of one or two topics chosen for intensive study by the candidate and approved by the Head of Department. Examination is by dissertation; an oral examination may be required. The Department has research programmes in four particular areas of Applied Mathematics: (i) general relativity and astrophysics; (ii) mathematical modelling of biological, ecological and environmental systems; (iii) continuum mechanics, applied analysis and finite elements; and (iv) nonlinear evolution equations and non-integrable systems. Candidates are particularly encouraged to take part in one of these programmes.
assessment: Examination by dissertation; oral examination may also be required.
